Wage Rate RequirementsFor provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of , including authority of Secretary of Labor, see , The Public Health and Welfare.Pub. L. 117–58section 18851 of Title 42

children.heading: Payment; rate
children.label: a Payment; rate
children.text: All operators of coal mining operations subject to the provisions of this chapter shall pay to the Secretary of the Interior, for deposit in the fund, a reclamation fee of 22.4 cents per ton of coal produced by surface coal mining and 9.6 cents per ton of coal produced by underground mining or 10 per centum of the value of the coal at the mine, as determined by the Secretary, whichever is less, except that the reclamation fee for lignite coal shall be at a rate of 2 per centum of the value of the coal at the mine, or 6.4 cents per ton, whichever is less.

children.heading: Due date
children.label: b Due date
children.text: Such fee shall be paid no later than thirty days after the end of each calendar quarter beginning with the first calendar quarter occurring after , and ending .August 3, 1977September 30, 2034

children.heading: Submission of statement
children.label: c Submission of statement
children.text: Together with such reclamation fee, all operators of coal mine operations shall submit a statement of the amount of coal produced during the calendar quarter, the method of coal removal and the type of coal, the accuracy of which shall be sworn to by the operator and notarized. Such statement shall include an identification of the permittee of the surface coal mining operation, any operator in addition to the permittee, the owner of the coal, the preparation plant, tripple, or loading point for the coal, and the person purchasing the coal from the operator. The report shall also specify the number of the permit required under and the mine safety and health identification number. Each quarterly report shall contain a notification of any changes in the information required by this subsection since the date of the preceding quarterly report. The information contained in the quarterly reports under this subsection shall be maintained by the Secretary in a computerized database.1section 1256 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “tipple,”.1

Any person, corporate officer, agent or director, on behalf of a coal mine operator, who knowingly makes any false statement, representation or certification, or knowingly fails to make any statement, representation or certification required in this section shall, upon conviction, be punished by a fine of not more than $10,000, or by imprisonment for not more than one year, or both.

The Secretary shall conduct such audits of coal production and the payment of fees under this subchapter as may be necessary to ensure full compliance with the provisions of this subchapter. For purposes of performing such audits the Secretary (or any duly designated officer, employee, or representative of the Secretary) shall, at all reasonable times, upon request, have access to, and may copy, all books, papers, and other documents of any person subject to the provisions of this subchapter. The Secretary may at any time conduct audits of any surface coal mining and reclamation operation, including without limitation, tipples and preparation plants, as may be necessary in the judgment of the Secretary to ensure full and complete payment of the fees under this subchapter.

children.heading: Civil action to recover fee
children.label: e Civil action to recover fee
children.text: Any portion of the reclamation fee not properly or promptly paid pursuant to this section shall be recoverable, with statutory interest, from coal mine operators, in any court of competent jurisdiction in any action at law to compel payment of debts.

children.heading: Cooperation from other agencies
children.label: f Cooperation from other agencies
children.text: All Federal and State agencies shall fully cooperate with the Secretary of the Interior in the enforcement of this section. Whenever the Secretary believes that any person has not paid the full amount of the fee payable under subsection (a) the Secretary shall notify the Federal agency responsible for ensuring compliance with the provisions of .section 4121 of title 26

children.text: The funds allocated by the Secretary under this paragraph to States and Indian tribes shall only be used for annual reclamation project construction and program administration grants.

children.label: D
children.text: To the extent not expended within 3 years after the date of any grant award under this paragraph (except for grants awarded during fiscal years 2008, 2009, and 2010 to the extent not expended within 5 years), such grant shall be available for expenditure by the Secretary under paragraph (5).

In making the grants referred to in paragraph (1)(C) and the grants referred to in paragraph (5), the Secretary shall ensure strict compliance by the States and Indian tribes with the priorities described in until a certification is made under .section 1233(a) of this titlesection 1240a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Amounts available in the fund which are not allocated under paragraphs (1), (2), and (5) or expended under paragraph (3) in any fiscal year are authorized to be expended by the Secretary under this paragraph for the reclamation or drainage abatement of lands and waters within unreclaimed sites which are mined for coal or which were affected by such mining, wastebanks, coal processing or other coal mining processes and left in an inadequate reclamation status.

children.label: i
children.text: A finding that the surface coal mining operation occurred during the period beginning on , and ending on or before the date on which the Secretary approved a State program pursuant to for a State in which the site is located, and that any funds for reclamation or abatement which are available pursuant to a bond or other form of financial guarantee or from any other source are not sufficient to provide for adequate reclamation or abatement at the site.August 4, 1977section 1253 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: In determining which sites to reclaim pursuant to this paragraph, the Secretary shall follow the priorities stated in paragraphs (1) and (2) of . The Secretary shall ensure that priority is given to those sites which are in the immediate vicinity of a residential area or which have an adverse economic impact upon a local community.section 1233(a) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: Amounts collected from the assessment of civil penalties under are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this paragraph.section 1268 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Any State may expend grants made available under paragraphs (1) and (5) for reclamation and abatement of any site referred to in subparagraph (A) if the State, with the concurrence of the Secretary, makes either of the findings referred to in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B) and if the State determines that the reclamation priority of the site is the same or more urgent than the reclamation priority for eligible lands and waters pursuant to under the priorities stated in paragraphs (1) and (2) of .section 1234 of this titlesection 1233(a) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: For the purposes of the certification referred to in , sites referred to in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph shall be considered as having the same priorities as those stated in for eligible lands and waters pursuant to . All sites referred to in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph within any State shall be reclaimed prior to such State making the certification referred to in .section 1240a(a) of this titlesection 1233(a) of this titlesection 1234 of this titlesection 1240a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate 60 percent of the amount in the fund after making the allocation referred to in paragraph (1) for making additional annual grants to States and Indian tribes which are not certified under to supplement grants received by such States and Indian tribes pursuant to paragraph (1)(C) until the priorities stated in paragraphs (1) and (2) of have been achieved by such State or Indian tribe. The allocation of such funds for the purpose of making such expenditures shall be through a formula based on the amount of coal historically produced in the State or from the Indian lands concerned prior to . Funds made available under paragraph (3) or (4) of this subsection for any State or Indian tribe shall not be deducted against any allocation of funds to the State or Indian tribe under paragraph (1) or under this paragraph.section 1240a(a) of this titlesection 1233(a) of this titleAugust 3, 1977

children.label: B
children.text: Any amount that is reallocated and available under shall be in addition to amounts that are allocated under subparagraph (A).section 1240a(h)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Any State with an approved abandoned mine reclamation program pursuant to may receive and retain, without regard to the 3-year limitation referred to in paragraph (1)(D), up to 30 percent of the total of the grants made annually to the State under paragraphs (1) and (5) if those amounts are deposited into an acid mine drainage abatement and treatment fund established under State law, from which amounts (together with all interest earned on the amounts) are expended by the State for the abatement of the causes and the treatment of the effects of acid mine drainage in a comprehensive manner within qualified hydrologic units affected by coal mining practices.section 1235 of this title

In complying with the priorities described in , any State or Indian tribe may use amounts available in grants made annually to the State or tribe under paragraphs (1) and (5) for the reclamation of eligible land and water described in before the completion of reclamation projects under paragraphs (1) and (2) of only if the expenditure of funds for the reclamation is done in conjunction with the expenditure before, on, or after , of funds for reclamation projects under paragraphs (1) and (2) of .section 1233(a) of this titlesection 1233(a)(3) of this titlesection 1233(a) of this titlesection 1233(a) of this titleDecember 20, 2006

children.label: A
children.text: In making funds available under this subchapter, the Secretary shall ensure that the grant awards total not less than $3,000,000 annually to each State and each Indian tribe having an approved abandoned mine reclamation program pursuant to and eligible land and water pursuant to , so long as an allocation of funds to the State or tribe is necessary to achieve the priorities stated in paragraphs (1) and (2) of .section 1235 of this titlesection 1234 of this titlesection 1233(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, this paragraph applies to the States of Tennessee and Missouri.

children.heading: Transfers of interest earned by Fund
children.label: h Transfers of interest earned by Fund

children.heading: Transfers to Combined Benefit Fund
children.label: A Transfers to Combined Benefit Fund
children.text: As soon as practicable after the beginning of fiscal year 2007 and each fiscal year thereafter, and before making any allocation with respect to the fiscal year under subsection (g), the Secretary shall use an amount not to exceed the amount of interest that the Secretary estimates will be earned and paid to the fund during the fiscal year to transfer to the Combined Benefit Fund such amounts as are estimated by the trustees of such fund to offset the amount of any deficit in net assets in the Combined Benefit Fund as of , and to make the transfer described in paragraph (2)(A).October 1, 2006

children.heading: Transfers to 1992 and 1993 plans
children.label: B Transfers to 1992 and 1993 plans
children.text: As soon as practicable after the beginning of fiscal year 2008 and each fiscal year thereafter, and before making any allocation with respect to the fiscal year under subsection (g), the Secretary shall use an amount not to exceed the amount of interest that the Secretary estimates will be earned and paid to the fund during the fiscal year (reduced by the amount used under subparagraph (A)) to make the transfers described in paragraphs (2)(B) and (2)(C).

children.heading: Eligibility of certain retirees
children.label: iii Eligibility of certain retirees
children.text: Individuals referred to in clause (ii)(II) shall be treated as eligible to receive health benefits under the Plan.

children.heading: Requirements for transfer
children.label: iv Requirements for transfer
children.text: The amount of the transfer otherwise determined under this subparagraph for a fiscal year shall be reduced by any amount transferred for the fiscal year to the Plan, to pay benefits required under the Plan, from a voluntary employees’ beneficiary association established as a result of a bankruptcy proceeding described in clause (ii).

children.heading: VEBA transfer
children.label: v VEBA transfer
children.text: The administrator of such voluntary employees’ beneficiary association shall transfer to the Plan any amounts received as a result of such bankruptcy proceeding, reduced by an amount for administrative costs of such association.

children.label: I
children.text: is a signatory operator; or

children.label: II
children.text: is or was a debtor in a bankruptcy proceeding that was consolidated, administratively or otherwise, with the bankruptcy proceeding of a signatory operator or a related person to a signatory operator (as those terms are defined in ).section 9701(c) of title 26

children.heading: Individuals considered enrolled
children.label: D Individuals considered enrolled
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (C), any individual who was eligible to receive benefits from the Plan as of , even though benefits were being provided to the individual pursuant to a settlement agreement approved by order of a bankruptcy court entered on or before , will be considered to be actually enrolled in the Plan and shall receive benefits from the Plan beginning on .December 20, 2006September 30, 2004December 31, 2006

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: 3 Adjustment
children.text: If, for any fiscal year, the amount of a transfer under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (2) is more or less than the amount required to be transferred under that subparagraph, the Secretary shall appropriately adjust the amount transferred under that subparagraph for the next fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be held in reserve by the Secretary until such time as necessary to make the payments under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (i)(1), as described in clause (ii); and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the event that the amounts described in subsection (i)(1) are insufficient to make the maximum payments described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (i)(1), shall be used by the Secretary to supplement the payments so that the maximum amount permitted under those paragraphs is paid.

children.heading: Previously allocated amounts
children.label: B Previously allocated amounts
children.text: All amounts allocated under subsection (g)(2) before , for the program described in , but not appropriated before , shall be available to the Secretary to make the transfers described in paragraph (2).December 20, 2006December 20, 2006section 1236 of this title

children.label: C Adequacy of previously credited interest
i
children.text: consult with the trustees of the plans described in paragraph (2) at reasonable intervals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: notify Congress if a determination is made that the amounts held in reserve under subparagraph (A) are insufficient to meet future requirements under subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Additional reserve amounts
children.label: D Additional reserve amounts
children.text: In addition to amounts held in reserve under subparagraph (A), there is authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for transfer to the fund to carry out the purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Inapplicability of cap
children.label: E Inapplicability of cap
children.text: The limitation described in subsection (i)(3)(A) shall not apply to payments made from the reserve fund under this paragraph.

children.heading: Availability of funds for next fiscal year
children.label: A Availability of funds for next fiscal year
children.text: The Secretary may make transfers under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2) for a calendar year only if the Secretary determines, using actuarial projections provided by the trustees of the Combined Benefit Fund referred to in paragraph (2)(A), that amounts will be available under paragraph (1), after the transfer, for the next fiscal year for making the transfer under paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Rate of contributions of obligors
children.label: B Rate of contributions of obligors

children.heading: Rate
children.label: I Rate
children.text: A transfer under paragraph (2)(C) shall not be made for a calendar year unless the persons that are obligated to contribute to the plan referred to in paragraph (2)(C) on the date of the transfer are obligated to make the contributions at rates that are no less than those in effect on the date which is 30 days before .December 20, 2006

children.heading: Application
children.label: II Application
children.text: The contributions described in subclause (I) shall be applied first to the provision of benefits to those plan beneficiaries who are not described in paragraph (2)(C)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: From , through , the persons that, on , are obligated to contribute to the plan referred to in paragraph (2)(C) shall be obligated, collectively, to make contributions equal to the amount described in paragraph (2)(C), less the amount actually transferred due to the operation of subparagraph (C).December 20, 2006December 31, 2010December 20, 2006

children.heading: First calendar year
children.label: II First calendar year
children.text: Calendar year 2006 is the first calendar year for which contributions are required under this clause.

children.heading: Amount of contribution for 2006
children.label: III Amount of contribution for 2006
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (IV), the amount described in paragraph (2)(C) for calendar year 2006 shall be calculated as if paragraph (2)(C) had been in effect during 2005.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: IV Limitation
children.text: The contributions required under this clause for calendar year 2006 shall not exceed the amount necessary for solvency of the plan described in paragraph (2)(C), measured as of , and taking into account all assets held by the plan as of that date.December 31, 2006

children.heading: Division
children.label: iii Division
children.text: The collective annual contribution obligation required under clause (ii) shall be divided among the persons subject to the obligation, and applied uniformly, based on the hours worked for which contributions referred to in clause (i) would be owed.

children.label: i
children.text: For calendar year 2008, the Secretary shall make transfers equal to 25 percent of the amounts that would otherwise be required under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2).

children.label: ii
children.text: For calendar year 2009, the Secretary shall make transfers equal to 50 percent of the amounts that would otherwise be required under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2).

children.label: iii
children.text: For calendar year 2010, the Secretary shall make transfers equal to 75 percent of the amounts that would otherwise be required under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2).

children.label: B

children.heading: Payments to States and Indian tribes
children.label: 2 Payments to States and Indian tribes
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary of the Interior for distribution to States and Indian tribes such sums as are necessary to pay amounts described in paragraphs (1)(A) and (2)(A) of .section 1240a(h) of this title

children.heading: Cap
children.label: A Cap
children.text: The total amount transferred under this subsection for any fiscal year shall not exceed $750,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: each such transfer for the fiscal year is a percentage of the amount described;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount is determined without regard to subsection (h)(5)(A); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the percentage transferred is the same for all transfers made under paragraph (1) for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Increase in limitation to account for calculation of health benefit plan excess
children.label: C Increase in limitation to account for calculation of health benefit plan excess
children.text: The dollar limitation under subparagraph (A) shall be increased by the amount of the cost to provide benefits which are taken into account under subsection (h)(2)(C)(ii) solely by reason of the amendments made by section 2(a) of the American Miner Benefits Improvement Act of 2020.

children.heading: Calculation
children.label: A Calculation
children.text: If the dollar limitation specified in paragraph (3)(A) exceeds the aggregate amount required to be transferred under paragraphs (1) and (2) for a fiscal year, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer an additional amount equal to the difference between such dollar limitation and such aggregate amount to the trustees of the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan to pay benefits required under that plan.

children.heading: Cessation of transfers
children.label: B Cessation of transfers
children.text: The transfers described in subparagraph (A) shall cease as of the first fiscal year beginning after the first plan year for which the funded percentage (as defined in ) of the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan is at least 100 percent.section 432(j)(2) of title 26

children.heading: Prohibition on benefit increases, etc.
children.label: C Prohibition on benefit increases, etc.
children.text: During a fiscal year in which the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan is receiving transfers under subparagraph (A), no amendment of such plan which increases the liabilities of the plan by reason of any increase in benefits, any change in the accrual of benefits, or any change in the rate at which benefits become nonforfeitable under the plan may be adopted unless the amendment is required as a condition of qualification under part I of subchapter D of chapter 1 of title 26 [ et seq.].26 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Critical status to be maintained
children.label: D Critical status to be maintained
i
children.text: the Plan shall be treated as if it were in critical status for purposes of sections 412(b)(3), 432(e)(3), and 4971(g)(1)(A) of title 26 and sections 1082(b)(3) and 1085(e)(3) of title 29;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Plan shall maintain and comply with its rehabilitation plan under section 432(e) of such Code and , including any updates thereto; andsection 1085(e) of title 29

children.label: iii
children.text: the provisions of subsections (c) and (d) of section 432 of such Code and subsections (c) and (d) of shall not apply.section 1085 of title 29

children.heading: Treatment of transfers for purposes of withdrawal liability under ERISA
children.label: E Treatment of transfers for purposes of withdrawal liability under ERISA
children.text: The amount of any transfer made under subparagraph (A) (and any earnings attributable thereto) shall be disregarded in determining the unfunded vested benefits of the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan and the allocation of such unfunded vested benefits to an employer for purposes of determining the employer’s withdrawal liability under .section 1381 of title 29

children.heading: Requirement to maintain contribution rate
children.label: F Requirement to maintain contribution rate
children.text: A transfer under subparagraph (A) shall not be made for a fiscal year unless the persons that are obligated to contribute to the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan on the date of the transfer are obligated to make the contributions at rates that are no less than those in effect on the date which is 30 days before .December 20, 2019

children.label: I
children.text: whether the plan is in endangered or critical status under and as of the first day of such plan year;section 1085 of title 29section 432 of title 26

children.label: II
children.text: the funded percentage (as defined in ) as of the first day of such plan year, and the underlying actuarial value of assets and liabilities taken into account in determining such percentage;section 432(j)(2) of title 26

children.label: III
children.text: the market value of the assets of the plan as of the last day of the plan year preceding such plan year;

children.label: IV
children.text: the total value of all contributions made during the plan year preceding such plan year;

children.label: V
children.text: the total value of all benefits paid during the plan year preceding such plan year;

children.label: VI
children.text: cash flow projections for such plan year and either the 6 or 10 succeeding plan years, at the election of the trustees, and the assumptions relied upon in making such projections;

children.label: VII
children.text: funding standard account projections for such plan year and the 9 succeeding plan years, and the assumptions relied upon in making such projections;

children.label: VIII
children.text: the total value of all investment gains or losses during the plan year preceding such plan year;

children.label: IX
children.text: any significant reduction in the number of active participants during the plan year preceding such plan year, and the reason for such reduction;

children.label: X
children.text: a list of employers that withdrew from the plan in the plan year preceding such plan year, and the resulting reduction in contributions;

children.label: XI
children.text: a list of employers that paid withdrawal liability to the plan during the plan year preceding such plan year and, for each employer, a total assessment of the withdrawal liability paid, the annual payment amount, and the number of years remaining in the payment schedule with respect to such withdrawal liability;

children.label: XII
children.text: any material changes to benefits, accrual rates, or contribution rates during the plan year preceding such plan year;

children.label: XIII
children.text: any scheduled benefit increase or decrease in the plan year preceding such plan year having a material effect on liabilities of the plan;

children.label: XIV
children.text: details regarding any funding improvement plan or rehabilitation plan and updates to such plan;

children.label: XV
children.text: the number of participants and beneficiaries during the plan year preceding such plan year who are active participants, the number of participants and beneficiaries in pay status, and the number of terminated vested participants and beneficiaries;

children.label: XVI
children.text: the information contained on the most recent annual funding notice submitted by the plan under ;section 1021(f) of title 29

children.label: XVII
children.text: the information contained on the most recent Department of Labor Form 5500 of the plan; and

children.label: XVIII
children.text: copies of the plan document and amendments, other retirement benefit or ancillary benefit plans relating to the plan and contribution obligations under such plans, a breakdown of administrative expenses of the plan, participant census data and distribution of benefits, the most recent actuarial valuation report as of the plan year, copies of collective bargaining agreements, and financial reports, and such other information as the Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor and the Director of the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, may require.

children.heading: Electronic submission
children.label: ii Electronic submission
children.text: The report required under clause (i) shall be submitted electronically.

children.heading: Information sharing
children.label: iii Information sharing
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate shall share the information in the report under clause (i) with the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: iv Penalty
children.text: Any failure to file the report required under clause (i) on or before the date described in such clause shall be treated as a failure to file a report required to be filed under , except that shall be applied with respect to any such failure by substituting “$100” for “$25”. The preceding sentence shall not apply if the Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate determines that reasonable diligence has been exercised by the trustees of such plan in attempting to timely file such report.section 6058(a) of title 26section 6652(e) of title 26

children.heading: 1974 UMWA Pension Plan defined
children.label: H 1974 UMWA Pension Plan defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “1974 UMWA Pension Plan” has the meaning given the term in , but without regard to the limitation on participation to individuals who retired in 1976 and thereafter.section 9701(a)(3) of title 26

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 5 Availability of funds
children.text: Funds shall be transferred under paragraphs (1) and (2) beginning in fiscal year 2008 and each fiscal year thereafter, and shall remain available until expended.
